To find the characteristic polynomial of the matrix $A$, we use the formula:
$$ p(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I) $$
where $I$ is the identity matrix.

Given the matrix:
$$ A = \left(\begin{array}{cc} -\frac{5}{2} & -\frac{5}{4} \\ 8 & \frac{27}{2} \end{array}\right) $$

**Step 1: Set up the matrix $A - \lambda I$.**
$$ A - \lambda I = \left(\begin{array}{cc} -\frac{5}{2} - \lambda & -\frac{5}{4} \\ 8 & \frac{27}{2} - \lambda \end{array}\right) $$

**Step 2: Calculate the determinant.**
The determinant of a $2 \times 2$ matrix $\left(\begin{array}{cc} a & b \\ c & d \end{array}\right)$ is $ad - bc$.
$$ \det(A - \lambda I) = \left(-\frac{5}{2} - \lambda\right)\left(\frac{27}{2} - \lambda\right) - \left(-\frac{5}{4}\right)(8) $$

**Step 3: Expand the terms.**
First, expand the product of the diagonal terms:
$$ \left(-\frac{5}{2} - \lambda\right)\left(\frac{27}{2} - \lambda\right) = \left(-\frac{5}{2}\right)\left(\frac{27}{2}\right) + \left(-\frac{5}{2}\right)(-\lambda) + (-\lambda)\left(\frac{27}{2}\right) + (-\lambda)(-\lambda) $$
$$ = -\frac{135}{4} + \frac{5}{2}\lambda - \frac{27}{2}\lambda + \lambda^2 $$
Combine the $\lambda$ terms:
$$ \frac{5}{2}\lambda - \frac{27}{2}\lambda = \frac{5 - 27}{2}\lambda = -\frac{22}{2}\lambda = -11\lambda $$
